response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with impressive systems-performance experience (low-latency asynchronous services, high-throughput data integration) and staff-level roles, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirement of current, hands-on C++ development for industrial automation. His C++ work dates to 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis; his recent decade centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/full-stack product work. He shows no exposure to MQTT, message brokers in an industrial context, IEC 62443, SDL practices, or OT/industrial protocols, and C++ is absent from his own skills list.
strengths: Proven high-performance systems work: low-latency services handling millions of requests/second using async I/O, fibers, and caching
Broad, senior engineering background across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, with strong testing/TDD craftsmanship and mentoring experience
Some relevant adjacent experience: C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI and event-driven, geo-data platform architecture
concerns: C++/STL experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; recent work is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, and C++ is not listed in his skills
No industrial automation, IoT/MQTT, IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial-protocol (Modbus, OPC UA, DNP3) experience
Career trajectory points toward full-stack/web and developer tooling, raising doubts about fit and retention in an on-prem industrial C++ role

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or IEC 62443/SDL background
Strong concurrent, low-latency systems engineering skills
